solutions.solution: Answer: (d) Blocks, Metals, Glasses, Frames diagram shown with conclusions: I. All blocks are either metals or glasses (✗), II. Some blocks are metals (✗), III. Some frames are blocks (✓)
Answer: (e) Stones, Dews, Drops diagram shown with conclusions: I. Atleast some dews are stones (✓), II. Atleast some stones are drops (✓)

Answer: (b) Philosophers, Fools, Illiterate diagram shown with conclusions: I. All Philosophers are illiterate (✓), II. All illiterates are Philosopher (✗), III. All illiterates are fools (✗), IV. Some illiterates would be Philosopher (✓)
Answer: (e) Beads, Bangles, Rings, Clothes, Boxes diagram shown with conclusions: I. Some boxes are bangles (✓), II. Some clothes are rings (✓), III. Some bangles are beads (✗)
Answer: (c) Chairs, Winds, Clocks, Fires, Nets diagram shown with conclusions: I. Some clocks are winds (✗), II. Some nets are fires (✓), III. Some winds are chairs (✗)
Answer: (c) Desks, Squares, Pillars, Rectangles, Circles diagram shown with conclusions: I. Some rectangles are pillars (✗), II. Some circles are desks (✓), III. Some squares are desks (✗)
Answer: (q) Line, Square, Circles, Triangle diagram shown with conclusions: I. All squares can never be triangles (✓), II. Some lines are circles (✗)
Answer: (e) with conclusions: I. No triangles is a square (✓), II. No line is a circle (✓)
Answer: (e) Songs, Paragraph, Poems, Rhymes diagram shown with conclusions: I. No song is a paragraph (✓), II. No poem is a paragraph (✓)
Answer: (b) with conclusions: I. All rhymes are poems (✗), II. All songs are rhymes (✓)
